fact: June 6 is exactly 157 days into the year, which means there are only 208 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, the sun rises super early on June 6 - sometimes before 5:30 AM in places like New York!
Fireflies start their magical light shows around this time of year in North America, blinking their butts to find mates!
June 6 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season, when people born under this zodiac sign are supposed to be extra chatty and curious.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, June 6 marks the heart of winter, with temperatures dropping and leaves falling off trees.
The sun sets after 8 PM in many northern cities on this date, giving kids extra daylight hours to play outside after dinner!
June 6 sits just two weeks before the summer solstice, making it one of the longest days of the year in terms of daylight hours.
Strawberry season peaks around this time in many parts of North America, making the berries extra sweet and juicy!
In northern regions like Alaska, the sun barely sets at all on June 6, creating an eerie twilight that lasts almost all night long.
Baby deer called fawns are wobbling around on their skinny legs right around this time of year, having been born in late spring.
The moon phase on June 6 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it appears lower in the sky than winter full moons.
June 6 marks peak allergy season in many places, when tree pollen makes people sneeze like crazy and covers cars in yellow dust!
